text.title: workflows Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for workflows as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agents drift into implementation during spec phase, solving HOW instead of defining WHAT.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents default to action-oriented thinking. Enforce "WHAT not HOW" strictly in the spec prompt and reject spec documents that contain architectural decisions (those belong in design).

failure-mode.description: Context window exhaustion mid-phase causes the agent to silently drop requirements, producing incomplete specs or designs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Break large features into sub-specs before execution begins. If a feature requires 60k tokens for the spec alone, split it into two or three smaller spec documents before entering design.

failure-mode.description: The "one task, one focus" rule is violated: research ("figure out how") is combined with implementation ("build it") in a single agent call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents degrade when context is mixed between research and execution. These are two separate tasks with separate quality gates: (1) research phase outputs a design.md; (2) implementation phase executes it.

failure-mode.description: Phase transition confidence scores are self-assessed; an overconfident agent moves to design with an incomplete spec.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a reviewer sub-agent as a check. The orchestrating agent should invoke a second pass from a fresh agent with prompt "Review this spec for completeness against the acceptance criteria. List gaps." before proceeding.

failure-mode.description: `session.md` is written at phase boundaries but crashes occur between phases, leaving inconsistent state (session.md says "in design" but no design.md exists).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always re-read session.md at session start and validate that claimed artifacts exist. If they do not, ask the user whether to roll back to the previous phase or re-run the incomplete phase.

failure-mode.description: Workflow state stored in markdown files can diverge if multiple agents write concurrently without locking.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use file-based locking (flock) when multiple agents run in parallel. The primary agent acquires a lock on .aidocs/memory/session.md before reading or writing it.
